chunk_summary: Guidelines for initiating, advancing, and weaning donor human milk (DHM) in preterm infants, emphasizing early fortification, maternal milk transition, and lactation support during weaning.
learning_objectives: Understand the initiation protocols for donor human milk in preterm infants.
Learn feeding advancement rates and fortification guidelines for DHM.
Identify criteria for transitioning from donor milk to maternal milk or formula.
Recognize the importance of lactation support during the weaning phase.
key_facts: DHM initiation often begins at trophic volumes (e.g., 20–30 mL/kg/day) on day 1–2 of life.
Multi-nutrient fortification is recommended once feeds reach 80–100 mL/kg/day for DHM.
Donor milk typically has lower protein and energy density due to pasteurization and later-lactation donors.
Weaning criteria include gestational maturity (e.g., 34–36 weeks postmenstrual age), stable cardiorespiratory status, and reliable maternal milk supply.
Transition from DHM to maternal milk should be stepwise, monitoring tolerance and weight gain.
reference_quote: Initiate pasteurized DHM as soon as enteral feeding is indicated when mother’s milk is unavailable or insufficient, particularly for very preterm infants. (WHO recommendations for care of the preterm or low-birth-weight infant)
document_key_topics: Donor human milk initiation and feeding advancement
Transitioning from donor milk to maternal milk
